topic: An athlete doesn't make the team they expected to join
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the locker room hung thick and stale, a suffocating blanket mirroring the knot tightening in Elias’s chest. He stared at the roster posted on the corkboard, his knuckles white where he gripped his duffel bag. His name wasn't there. Not a single name that represented the team he had bled for, the team he had sacrificed everything for. A low, guttural sound escaped his throat, a sound more animal than human. He slammed the bag onto the bench, the impact rattling the metal lockers. "Unbelievable," he muttered, the word tasting like ash. He spun around, his eyes blazing with a cold, hard intensity that made the few other athletes shrink back. He didn't bother to pack; he just walked out, the sound of his heavy boots echoing the violent rhythm of his disappointment.

[story 2]

"This is a joke," Sarah hissed, her voice tight with suppressed agitation, as she stared at the team announcement on her phone. Liam felt a heat rising up his neck, a physical pressure building behind his eyes. He clenched his jaw so hard he could feel the tendons strain. He paced the edge of the court, his movements jerky and sharp. Every breath felt shallow, inadequate. He stopped abruptly, kicking a discarded water bottle across the floor with unnecessary force. "They just don't see it," he ground out, his voice dangerously low, vibrating with contained energy. He ran a hand through his sweat-dampened hair, pulling it back roughly. The injustice of it all was a sharp, stinging sensation, demanding an immediate, explosive release.

[story 3]

Mark stood by the window, the afternoon sun casting long, accusing shadows across the empty field. He watched the team practice, their coordinated movements a mockery of his own solitary frustration. A deep, churning anger settled in his gut, heavy and unwelcome. He clenched his fists until his nails dug crescents into his palms, the skin protesting with sharp pain. He turned from the glass, his gaze sweeping over the empty stands, a look of pure, unadulterated resentment etched onto his face. "They had no choice," he whispered, the words laced with a bitterness that made his teeth ache. He walked towards the exit, each step deliberate, heavy with the weight of things left unsaid and opportunities brutally denied.
